surface.name: LiveKit Voice AI quickstart through the first browser Agent Console response
surface.selection_basis: The current documentation home leads developers to voice and video AI agents, and the Build Agents navigation labels Voice AI quickstart as a Get Started page. That guide explicitly promises a simple voice assistant in less than 10 minutes and says the CLI starter is the simplest way to get a first agent running.

alternatives_considered.name: Agent Builder
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The quickstart describes it as a browser-only prototyping route with fewer features than the Agents SDK.

alternatives_considered.name: Node.js starter
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer tab. The Python path is the guide's first tab and supports terminal console mode in addition to browser testing.

alternatives_considered.name: Self-hosted LiveKit
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The voice quickstart assumes LiveKit Cloud and treats self-hosting as an adaptation with additional deployment and model-provider changes.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a first LiveKit Cloud account and project, initialize the official Python starter, run it in development mode, and speak to it in Agent Console until a response appears.
assumed_prior_state: No LiveKit Cloud account, project, API keys, CLI, starter directory, dependencies, running agent, browser session, or prior LiveKit usage is assumed.

Python 3.10 or newer, uv, a shell, browser, microphone, and permission to install the CLI are available; no signup, terms acceptance, authentication, project creation, package installation, model inference, or session was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Not established end to end by public official documentation
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The Voice AI quickstart cannot be reconstructed from a no-account state because the public Cloud login page exposes identity choices but not the email-link or identity-provider completion states that lead to an account and free project.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Cloud page offers Continue with Google, Continue with GitHub, Continue with SSO, and Continue with email under Request a link to sign in or sign up, then requires an unenumerated continuation.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The required account-registration transition after a LiveKit Cloud identity choice is not established by public official documentation.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The quickstart fully documents the later CLI initialization, dependency installation, dev run, and Agent Console session, but reaching those steps requires a Cloud account and project whose registration sequence cannot be reconstructed without invention.

prerequisites.requirement: Sign up for a free LiveKit Cloud account through Google, GitHub, SSO, or an emailed link; the downstream registration states are not publicly enumerated.
By signing up, agree to LiveKit's terms of service and privacy policy.
Create a free LiveKit Cloud project and use its API keys for the quickstart.
Use Python 3.10 or newer with uv and install the LiveKit CLI.
Have a browser and microphone for the Agent Console voice session.
The Build plan is free and its included allowance is a hard cap, so a credit card is not established as a prerequisite to the first agent session.

primary_path.action: Open the official Voice AI quickstart and follow its LiveKit Cloud requirement.
primary_path.output: The guide identifies a free Cloud account, free project, Python and uv, and the LiveKit CLI as prerequisites.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI setup and first-agent sequence are visible.

primary_path.action: Open LiveKit Cloud, which redirects an unauthenticated user to Continue to LiveKit Cloud.
primary_path.output: Google, GitHub, SSO, and email-link authentication choices appear.
primary_path.success_signal: The login or registration choices are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The same page serves both sign-in and registration.

primary_path.action: Select Continue with email under Request a link to sign in or sign up.
primary_path.output: An email-link continuation is required to complete account access.
primary_path.success_signal: The initial account-entry choice has been made.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The public response and official documentation do not enumerate the email input, link delivery, link activation, profile fields, verification, account-completion state, or first-project creation screens that follow.

candidate_path_gap.reason: After the undocumented Cloud registration and project creation transitions, the official continuation is to install the CLI, run `lk cloud auth`, approve the browser link, run `lk agent init my-agent --template agent-starter-python`, select a project if prompted, save the printed Console link, enter `my-agent`, run `uv sync`, run `lk agent dev`, open the Console link, click Start a session, grant microphone access if prompted, speak, and observe the agent's realtime response.
candidate_path_gap.impact: The detailed code and runtime route cannot form an end-to-end first-user candidate while the mandatory account and first-project transitions remain hidden.

branches.condition: Use Google, GitHub, or SSO instead of email
branches.path: Select the matching provider and complete its external authentication flow.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the email-link route with a different undocumented identity continuation before converging on Cloud.

branches.path: Use Node.js 20 or newer, pnpm 10.15.0 or newer, the `agent-starter-node` template, and `pnpm install`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes runtime and dependency commands while preserving the same Agent Console terminal.

branches.condition: Use Agent Builder instead of code
branches.path: Prototype and test a voice agent in the browser without writing code.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Provides a faster no-code prototype but does not exercise the full Agents SDK quickstart.

branches.path: Choose the realtime-model agent code and supply `OPENAI_API_KEY`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an external provider credential; the documented default STT-LLM-TTS route uses LiveKit Inference with no additional model setup.

friction_gates.description: The required email-link or identity-provider registration continuation is not publicly enumerated.
The Cloud page states that signup agrees to the terms of service and privacy policy.
The CLI browser-auth flow generates and links project credentials before starter initialization.
The browser Agent Console requires microphone use for the documented voice interaction.
The free Build plan has hard-capped monthly allowances; requests fail after an allowance is exhausted.

sources.title: Sign in | LiveKit Cloud
evidence_supported: Google, GitHub, SSO, and email choices

sources.title: Project commands | LiveKit Documentation
evidence_supported: Automatic CLI API-key generation and project import

uncertainties.question: What exact email-link, identity-provider, profile, workspace, and first-project screens currently lead from the public Cloud login page to a usable free project?
uncertainties.impact: The mandatory account and resource-creation segment cannot be reconstructed or counted.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The public page exposes only the initial identity choices and the quickstart assumes that a free account and project already exist.

excluded_after_success: No complete first-success boundary is claimed while Cloud registration and first-project creation remain undocumented.

Custom prompts, alternative models, tests, deployment with `lk agent create`, telephony, custom frontends, production startup mode, and observability analysis occur after the first Agent Console response.
